name: Phoenix Lights
location: Henderson NV to Tucson AZ, USA
visual_description: Beginning around 7:30 PM MST, a formation of five to ten amber/white lights in a V-configuration was observed moving from Henderson Nevada southeastward across Arizona to Tucson. Ground witnesses described a massive craft — some estimating mile-wide — moving silently at low altitude, 100–300 feet overhead in some accounts. A Phoenix family reported the object passing directly overhead, occluding stars, with amber lights at each point of a carpenter's-square V-formation. Camcorder footage shows a formation of lights against the dark sky; the most famous footage shows a V of lights filmed from the Squaw Peak area that eventually extinguishes one by one from left to right — consistent either with flares on a para-chute sequentially released by National Guard A-10s (official explanation for the second event, ~10 PM) or lights shutting off sequentially on a single craft. The initial wave (7:30–8:45 PM), distinct from the later flare drop, has no conventional explanation.
authenticity_chain: 480i NTSC interlace, chroma bleed on amber lights against dark sky, VHS luma/chroma separation artifacts, gentle rolling noise, star-field background with light-pollution haze near horizon. V-formation of 7 amber point-lights with soft corona bloom at 30px radius.
cultural_significance: The most widely witnessed mass-UFO event in American history. Governor Symington called a press conference the next day and had an aide dressed in an alien costume brought out — he later admitted to investigators that he had also seen the formation from his car and was genuinely shaken. Frances Emma Barwood, the only Phoenix official who demanded a public investigation, was subsequently defeated in her next election. The event has been continuously re-examined for 25+ years.
notes: Two distinct events: the V-formation sighting (7:30–8:45 PM, no conventional explanation) and the A-10 flare drop over the Estrella Mountains (~10 PM, officially explained). Much of the famous camcorder footage documents the second event. The first event — the silent mile-wide formation with witness accounts of overhead pass and star occlusion — remains unexplained.
